judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very close in substance and evidence for (a), (b), and (d); Report 2 is stronger on (c), explicitly rejecting deferral of the doc fix and endorsing a same-commit/PR policy going forward, which Report 1 only partially addresses.

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... A grep for `chargekit` across `medibill/` finds exactly two call sites that bypass the gateway: `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16`... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13`... The only other provider import is `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6`, which imports just `chargekit.errors` exception classes — that is the boundary exception the doc explicitly permits, so it is not a deviation.

report_2.evidence: New refund calls should be a thin accessor (e.g. `refund(...)`) in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... naming `app/billing/errors.py` as the only exception (it imports only the SDK's error classes, `errors.py:6-9`, which matches its documented role)... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14-16`... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9-13`

report_1.evidence: In the code, `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`) has a sixth field, `external_ref`, which `charge_invoice` populates and returns (`backend/app/api/invoices.py:17-18`).

report_2.evidence: the actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`) has a sixth field, `external_ref`, which is populated by the charge route (`invoices.py:17`) and backed by migration `backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`.

drift between it and the code is a bug to fix now — not after the refund feature lands... Going forward, any change the refund feature makes to what the doc governs — a new gateway accessor, a new response field, a new webhook type — must update `ARCHITECTURE.md` in the same commit and be called out in that commit/PR summary.

report_1.evidence: hard-coding an exact count ("4 webhook event types") guarantees the doc rots on every addition. Better to enumerate the types without a count and point at `backend/app/api/webhooks.py` as the source of truth

report_2.evidence: a hard-coded count in prose is guaranteed to drift again. Drop the number entirely and either point readers at `backend/app/api/webhooks.py` as the source of truth for handled event types, or keep the list without asserting an exact count
